items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing for 333 Franklin Street postponed
items.summary: An administrative zoning hearing scheduled for April 8, 2026 has been cancelled, and the project at 333 Franklin Street will be rescheduled for a later date. The project will be re-noticed once a new hearing date is confirmed.

items.headline: Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project Moves Forward
items.summary: The city council approved a third amendment to the consultant agreement for the Bascom Avenue Highway-Rail Traffic Signal Project on April 7. This infrastructure project aims to improve traffic safety at the intersection of highway and rail crossings on Bascom Avenue.

items.headline: Local Streets Pavement Removal and Replacement Project Awarded
items.summary: City council awarded a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project on April 7, directing funding toward street repairs across San José. This project will address deteriorating pavement conditions in various neighborhoods.

items.headline: City Updates Active Transportation Plan with new scoring criteria
items.summary: The City Council received an update on Phase 2 of Cupertino's Active Transportation Plan, including revised scoring criteria and plan edits. This plan shapes how the city prioritizes biking and walking infrastructure projects over the coming years.

items.headline: City Council approves construction project with new contract award
items.summary: The council adopted plans and specifications for a construction project and awarded a contract, though the agenda does not specify which city infrastructure or facility is being built or improved. Residents may want to follow up to learn what project this involves.

items.headline: Milpitas considers changes to federal housing and community development plan
items.summary: The council reviewed a substantial amendment to the city's 2025-2030 Consolidated Plan, which governs how federal housing and community development funds are spent in Milpitas. This could affect affordable housing initiatives and neighborhood improvement priorities.

items.headline: Major senior housing complex approved in East San José
items.summary: Trillium senior apartments will add 65 units of affordable housing for seniors on E St John St, representing significant new supply for a growing demographic need in the area.

items.headline: New residential development underway on Gay Avenue corridor
items.summary: Multiple new homes and a duplex approved on Gay Avenue and N Capitol Avenue represent coordinated residential infill in this area.
